subject: UX & Research
title: Government IA and Accessibility
description: Learn Government IA and Accessibility in one minute.

hook.text: GOV.UK serves 330,000 pages to 11 million weekly visitors. It must work for everyone, including poor readers.

buildup.text: The Government Digital Service rebuilt the entire UK government web presence starting in 2012 with radical simplicity.

discovery.text: They organized by user need, not by department structure. Users don't know which department handles their tax question.

twist.text: GOV.UK's reading level target is age 9. Complex policies are rewritten in plain language anyone can understand.

climax.text: Task-oriented IA organized around 'I need to...' statements serves users better than 'About us' organizational charts.

punchline.text: Organize by user needs, not org structure. GOV.UK proved it works.

quiz.question: How did GOV.UK organize its information architecture?
options: By user needs and tasks, not by government department structure
By department hierarchy from the Prime Minister down
